stem:
A total of 40 students enrolled in a community dance class. The frequency table shows how many of these students train in each dance style.

Dance styleNumber of students
Ballet14
Jazz6
Contemporary10
Hip-hop10

If one of these students is selected at random, what is the probability that the student trains in contemporary dance?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The probability is the number of contemporary students divided by the total number of students. There are 10 contemporary students and 40 students total, so the probability is 10/40 = 0.25.

Choice A is incorrect; this would be the result if the class had 100 students. Choice B is incorrect; this is the probability of choosing a jazz student. Choice D is incorrect; this would be the result if the class had 25 students.
